name: xlrd (Excel XLS/XLSX Read Library)

performance.notes: Pure Python XLS parsing; speed depends on file size

agent_readiness.notes: Read legacy .xls files (Excel 97-2003 format). import xlrd. wb = xlrd.open_workbook('file.xls'). sheet = wb.sheet_by_index(0). sheet = wb.sheet_by_name('Sheet1'). sheet.nrows; sheet.ncols. cell = sheet.cell(row, col). cell.value. sheet.row_values(row_idx). wb.sheet_names(). CRITICAL: xlrd 2.0+ dropped XLSX support — XLS only. Use openpyxl for .xlsx files. Use pandas.read_excel() for both. xlrd v1.x supported .xlsx but was deprecated. Pandas: pd.read_excel('file.xls', engine='xlrd') for .xls files.

gotchas: xlrd 2.0+ ONLY reads .xls (Excel 97-2003) — NOT .xlsx! Use openpyxl for .xlsx
Many users still expect xlrd to read .xlsx — this is the #1 source of confusion
xlrd v1 (old) supported .xlsx; v2 removed it due to security concerns
For both formats: pandas.read_excel() auto-selects engine
Legacy format — new files should be .xlsx
best_when: Reading legacy .xls files from old Excel (97-2003 format)
Data pipelines processing older Excel exports
When pandas read_excel needs engine='xlrd' for .xls
avoid_when: New projects — generate .xlsx with openpyxl or xlsxwriter
When calamine (Rust-based) is available for faster reading
summary: xlrd reads legacy .xls Excel files (97-2003 format only). import xlrd; wb = xlrd.open_workbook('file.xls'); sheet = wb.sheet_by_index(0); cell.value. BSD-3. xlrd 2.0+ does NOT read .xlsx — use openpyxl for .xlsx. pandas.read_excel('file.xls', engine='xlrd') for xls. Legacy format — new files should use openpyxl.
